summary: Phase 2 (Super Admin platform) + Phase 3 (per-tenant branding) fully verified. Backend: 20/20 pytest pass — super login/list/create/plan/status/delete, tenant provisioning auto-creates 'Demo - Emma & James 01.01.26', branding GET/PUT/logo upload, public branding-asset serving, share meta returns branding object, tenant isolation (mark vs rose galleries + branding). Frontend: /super login + tenant list + create (dialog testids present) + toggle status + change plan via Radix select all work. /admin dashboard: dash-logo renders IMG when tenant has logo (alt='Weddings By Mark', src is /branding-asset/…) and SPAN with business name otherwise — no hardcoded /logo.png. Branding page persists tagline/email/accent_color (verified via reload + direct API check). Client share /s/gina-mark shows tenant brand (Weddings By Mark) and footer 'Site Designed & Hosted by / StudioApp'; /s/<rose-token> shows 'Rose Photography' and NO 'Weddings By Mark' leak — isolation confirmed.

issues: Logo preview container uses a dark #1C1917 background which hides dark logos; consider a subtle checkerboard or letting user toggle preview background.

critical_code_review_comments: AdminBranding.save() PUTs {business_name, accent_color, contact_email, tagline} without logo_url. This is fine because BrandingUpdate Pydantic model excludes logo_url (logo is only set via /admin/branding/logo). Just be aware if UI later needs to clear a logo — there is currently no 'remove logo' endpoint.

super_delete_tenant drops the tenant DB and deletes admins/share_index/tenant/upload dir — good — but does NOT invalidate outstanding JWTs for that tenant's admin. Deleted tenant admins with a still-valid JWT would hit get_admin -> lookup by tenant_id and 401 (because admin row is gone). Verified indirectly; acceptable.

super_list_tenants iterates use_tenant(t['id']) inside a request handler. Because use_tenant is per-request via context vars, sequential iteration is fine, but if this endpoint were ever awaited concurrently with another admin request the context leak could confuse. Consider using a scoped helper that just returns the count without mutating current_tenant_id.
get_tenant_branding uses control_db.tenants.find_one with projection {_id:0}. Good — no ObjectId leaks. Confirmed.

ShareView.js line 405: hero brand text color uses white when heroImageUrl or darkMode is truthy — fine — but if branding.business_name is empty AND no logo, header shows an empty span. Add fallback to 'StudioApp' like AdminDashboard does.

AdminBranding.js does not validate accent_color format client-side (any string is accepted). Backend also accepts anything. Consider a hex-pattern check to prevent CSS injection surface even though value is set via inline style.

admin_upload_logo reads full file into memory (5MB cap enforced) — acceptable for logos. Old logo files are NOT cleaned up when a new one is uploaded; branding_logo assets accumulate under UPLOAD_DIR/<tid>/.branding/. Consider deleting the previous file when overwritten.
test_credentials: Super admin: superadmin / Stu!d10App_2026xQ at /super. Tenant admins: mark / Test!2026 and rose / Rose!2026 at /admin. Public shares: /s/gina-mark (Mark) and /s/<rose-token> (Rose, generated during test).
seed_data_creation: Created & deleted one TEST tenant via /api/super/tenants during backend tests (business_name='TEST Studio <uuid>', username 'TEST_user_<uuid>'). Frontend also created a TEST tenant via UI which was left in place — main agent may delete 'TEST Photog xxxxxx' via super admin if desired. Mark's branding tagline/contact_email were left set to 'TEST_<hex>' and 'mark-test@studio.local' after the last successful save-persist test (harmless; can be blanked from /admin/branding).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Super admin creation via UI DOES call ct-submit successfully (verified). Radix Select for tenant plan renders items in a portal — click via 'div[role="option"]:has-text("…")' with force=True. Note: POST /api/admin/galleries/{gid}/shares requires body {"gallery_id": gid} (Pydantic ShareCreate has gallery_id as required). Frontend admin/api create-share UI passes it correctly; if testing via fetch/curl, include gallery_id. Rose's demo gallery was 'Demo - Emma & James 01.01.26' (auto-provisioned). The share meta endpoint /api/share/{token} returns {..., 'branding': {business_name, logo_url, accent_color, contact_email, tagline}} — ShareView reads this and updates state.
rca of the issue: No issues found. All Phase 2/3 features (super admin CRUD, tenant provisioning with demo gallery, per-tenant branding, share-page brand + StudioApp footer, mark/rose isolation) behave as specified.
